title: Wrong-way I-95 driver faces drunk driving charges after crash, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: John Tarshi, 25, from Lawrence, was arrested for drunk driving after crashing a Porsche on I-95 in Woburn.
Tarshi is potentially facing additional charges for possessing a forged New Hampshire RMV document found in the car.
Gov. Maura Healey has launched initiatives to reduce wrong-way driving accidents following a recent trooper's death.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming now (July 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jennifer Garner stars in 'The Five-Star Weekend,' debuting on Peacock July 9, filmed on Nantucket.
HBO Max adds Shark Week content starting July 26, featuring new shark shows and specials.
Prime Video releases Ryan Gosling's sci-fi movie 'Project Hail Mary' on July 3.

title: Noah Kahan’s historic 4-show Fenway Park run kicks off Tuesday. Here’s everything you need to know.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan begins a four-show series at Fenway Park in Boston, with performances on Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, and Saturday.
Each concert night will feature fireworks, weather permitting, as confirmed by the Boston Fire Department.
Local businesses offer Noah Kahan-themed items and special promotions, benefiting Kahan's mental health initiative, The Busyhead Project.

title: Labouré College cuts 65 jobs ahead of Curry College transition
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Labouré College will lay off 65 employees as part of its acquisition by Curry College, closing its Milton campus.
Labouré's nursing programs and student body will transition to Curry College, maintaining educational continuity for students.
Curry College will hire about 15 Labouré nursing faculty and 20 staff to support the new Labouré Center for Advancing Healthcare Opportunity.

title: Celtics sign Ron Harper Jr. to multi-year contract
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics sign guard Ron Harper Jr. to a multi-year contract worth up to $13.7 million over four years.
Harper Jr. averaged 4.2 points and 1.7 rebounds in 29 games for the Celtics this past season.
Ron Harper Jr., previously with the Raptors and Pistons, earned third-team All-NBA G-League honors with the Maine Celtics.

title: ‘Amateur hour’: Mass. sheriff accuses ICE of trying to bully him into breaking the law
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Bristol County Sheriff Paul Heroux accuses ICE of intimidation for criticizing his decision not to honor an ICE detainer.
ICE arrested Jose Raul Martinez-Alvarado in New Bedford, criticizing local authorities for releasing him after bail.
Heroux says honoring ICE detainers would violate Massachusetts law, citing court rulings that prevent such actions.

title: The murder of Karina Holmer remains unsolved 30 years later; police asking public for help
author: Emily Sweeney, The Boston Globe
bullets: Karina Holmer, a 20-year-old Swedish au pair, was murdered in Boston in 1996, case remains unsolved after 30 years.
Holmer's upper body was found in a dumpster on Boylston Street; her lower half remains missing.
Boston police urge the public to provide new information on the case, emphasizing the investigation is ongoing.

title: Brad Stevens ‘lost a lot of sleep’ over the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George, two first-round, and two second-round picks.
Brad Stevens stated the trade was difficult, citing salary cap concerns with Brown and Tatum's contracts.
Jaylen Brown expressed feeling disrespected in the trade process, despite open discussions with Stevens.

title: From ‘under the weather’ to ‘scuttlebutt’: The sailing phrases you’ll hear at Sail Boston 250
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Over 60 Tall Ships will be in Boston Harbor from July 11-16 for Sail Boston 250, with free public tours.
Visitors will encounter nautical terms like 'scuttlebutt', originating from sailors congregating around a water barrel.
Sail Boston 250 includes charter sails and showcases time-honored maritime traditions, such as manning the rails.

title: Bill Belichick was reportedly considered for a temporary head coach role with the Broncos
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Bill Belichick was considered for a temporary head coach role with the Broncos, as planned by Sean Payton.
The plan involved Belichick coaching until he achieved 15 wins, surpassing Don Shula's record of 347 career wins.
The arrangement was not pursued due to its complexity, and Belichick later joined the University of North Carolina.
